full_prompt: Design the recovery bay (coupler section housing the drogue and main parachute deployment system) of a student-built high-power sounding rocket competing in Launch Canada 2025, Advanced Flight category. Target apogee 20000 ft AGL (6096 m) with cumulative motor impulse at the FAA Class 2 ceiling of 40960 N*s. Vehicle GLOW 38 kg wet, airframe outer diameter 152 mm (6 inch), total length 3.2 m. The recovery bay is a separate tubular coupler section, length 600 mm, fitting inside adjacent airframe tubes by friction plus four M4 shear pins per interface (total 8 shear pins). Structure is 6061-T6 aluminum alloy tube (E 68.9 GPa, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3), wall thickness 2.5 mm. The bay contains: one 200 mm x 200 mm x 50 mm payload sled (4 kg of avionics), two pyrotechnic ejection charges (one drogue at apogee, one main at 300 m AGL per Launch Canada dual-event requirement), and a 2.5 m parachute for main event. Drogue canopy opening force peak is 1800 N; main canopy opening force peak is 4500 N. The bay has eyebolt attachments welded through internal bulkheads at each end. Load cases: (LC1) peak motor thrust transient: 4.5 kN axial on the bay from below + 380 N drag reaction on upper interface = compressive axial 4.12 kN on bay tube; (LC2) drogue deployment shock: 1800 N axial tension at lower eyebolt (bay hanging from rocket upper section at apogee); (LC3) main deployment shock: 4500 N axial tension at lower eyebolt during descent; (LC4) ejection pressure pulse: 70 kPa internal over 0.2 s, acting on bay inner wall between sealed bulkheads; (LC5) recovery bay impact if main fails to deploy: 60 m/s terminal with just drogue, equivalent static 25 g axial on the bay itself (this is a burst-margin / safe-life gate per Launch Canada airframe SF); (LC6) shear pin cross-check: verify bay-to-airframe interface shear pins fail before airframe yield during nominal separation (pins must shear at ~1000 N per DTEG). Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1-LC4, max von Mises stress in the bay tube must remain below 184 MPa (276 / 1.5 yield FoS, Launch Canada DTEG standard); (R2) linear buckling eigenvalue under LC1 axial compression must exceed 2.0; (R3) under LC5 burst case, stress must remain below 310 MPa (ultimate, no catastrophic failure) - implicit safe-life gate; (R4) bay dry mass (tube + 2 bulkheads + eyebolts) must not exceed 1.6 kg; (R5) under LC4, bulkhead deflection must remain below 2 mm (to preserve pyrotechnic initiator alignment); (R6) M4 shear pin total shear capacity at interface: 8 pins must not fail below 6000 N combined (per expected separation force) - verified by pin shear formula, not FEA. Vibration (random PSD) is recommended but not a pass/fail gate at this competition class; thermal, fluid, radiation not primary. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly, coupler + 2 bulkheads + 2 eyebolts + 8 shear pins as separate bodies, FEA report per LC1-LC5, buckling eigenvalue for LC1, bulkhead deflection for LC4, shear pin calculation, mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the recovery bay (coupler section housing the drogue + main parachute deployment system) of a student-built high-power sounding rocket for Launch Canada 2025 Advanced Flight category. Target apogee 20000 ft (6096 m); FAA Class 2 impulse ceiling 40960 N-s; GLOW 38 kg wet; airframe 152 mm OD x 3.2 m.

Envelope: 6061-T6 aluminum tube coupler, 600 mm long, 2.5 mm wall, friction-fit into adjacent airframe tubes plus 4 x M4 shear pins per interface (8 total); 200 x 200 x 50 mm avionics sled (4 kg); two pyro charges (drogue at apogee, main at 300 m AGL per Launch Canada dual event); 2.5 m main canopy (4500 N opening peak, 1800 N drogue peak); eyebolts welded through each end bulkhead.

Factor regime: Launch Canada DTEG yield FoS 1.5 (allowable 184 MPa) on LC1-LC4; linear buckling eigenvalue >= 2.0 under axial compression; LC5 burst-margin stress < ultimate (310 MPa); bulkhead deflection under pulse < 2 mm; 8 shear pins combined shear >= 6000 N per DTEG.

Load cases.
- Peak motor transient: 4.5 kN axial from below + 380 N drag above = 4.12 kN compression on the bay.
- Drogue shock: 1800 N axial tension at the lower eyebolt.
- Main shock: 4500 N axial tension at the lower eyebolt.
- Ejection pressure pulse: 70 kPa internal for 0.2 s between sealed bulkheads.
- Main-fails-to-deploy burst margin: 60 m/s drogue-only terminal = 25 g axial on the bay.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Shear-pin sizing for interface separation uses direct formula, not FEA.

prompt.title: Launch Canada Advanced-Flight Recovery Bay
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 coupler recovery bay, 152 mm OD x 600 mm L, 1800 N drogue + 4500 N main shock, DTEG FoS 1.5, 20000 ft apogee.

mission.recovery: dual-event: drogue at apogee + main at 300 m AGL

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell for tube; TET10 for bulkhead and eyebolt

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Random-vib PSD not a Launch Canada hard gate at this apogee class.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aero handled via thrust + drag axial combination LC1.
